items.title: Gemini is now available anywhere | Google Cloud Blog
items.author: cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Google Cloud has been active with Gemini AI announcements in early 2026. The headline release is Gemini 3.1 Pro, now available in preview on Vertex AI, Gemini Enterprise, Google AI Studio, Android Studio, and the Gemini CLI. Alongside it, Google introduced Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, positioned as the fastest and most cost-efficient model in the Gemini 3 series, optimized for high-volume developer workloads. Google also launched Gemini Enterprise as a new tier targeting business users with advanced c

items.title: Google brings Gemini 3 AI model to Search and AI Mode
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google has been actively advancing its Gemini AI model family through early 2026. The flagship release is Gemini 3, announced in April 2026, described as Google's most intelligent model combining reasoning, multimodality, and coding capabilities. Gemini 3 is available across the Gemini app, AI Studio, and Vertex AI, with a Deep Think variant designed for complex scientific and engineering problems available to Ultra subscribers. Gemini 3 Flash was also introduced as a faster, globally available

items.title: Google Unveils Major April 2026 Updates for Gemini AI
items.author: af.net
items.snippet: Google DeepMind (@GoogleDeepMind) has been active on X (Twitter) in April 2026 with several major announcements. Most notably, on approximately April 14, 2026, they announced an upgrade to Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 — touting significantly improved visual and spatial understanding to help robots reason about the physical world. They also announced a new research partnership with Boston Dynamics (January 2026) to integrate Gemini Robotics capabilities with Atlas humanoid robots, and a similar partner

items.title: Introducing Google Antigravity, a New Era in AI-Assisted Software Development
items.author: antigravity.google
items.snippet: Google Antigravity is Google's new agentic development platform launched in early 2026, designed to move developers beyond traditional code editing into a task-oriented, agent-first workflow. The platform combines a familiar AI-powered IDE (Editor View) with a Manager Surface where developers can spawn and orchestrate multiple agents working asynchronously across editor, terminal, and browser. Agents produce verifiable Artifacts — task lists, implementation plans, screenshots, and browser record

items.title: Google Gemini API free tier tightened: Pro models to become paid starting in April — Apiyi.com Blog
items.author: help.apiyi.com
items.snippet: Google's Gemini API has seen significant updates heading into April 2026. Major new model releases include Gemma 4 (gemma-4-26b-a4b-it and gemma-4-31b-it), Google's most capable open models purpose-built for agentic reasoning. The Gemini 3.x family expanded with Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview, Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview, and Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana 2). New agentic features include the Interactions API (Beta), the Gemini Deep Research Agent (preview), a File Search API for groundin

items.title: Gemini CLI v0.38.1: Subagents are here · Discussion #25562
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The google-gemini GitHub organization has been actively releasing updates in April 2026, with the Gemini CLI being the most prominent project. The latest notable release is Gemini CLI v0.38.1, which introduced public support for Subagents — allowing users to create custom expert agents via Markdown files with YAML frontmatter in ~/.gemini/agents. This release also brought context management improvements, new navigation commands (/about and /help via ACP), and a key input control change shifting

items.title: How to Download Firebase Studio Project [2026 Full Guide] - YouTube
items.author: www.youtube.com
items.snippet: Firebase Studio is Google's cloud-based agentic development environment that allows developers to build and deploy full-stack applications using AI assistance via Gemini. For game development, Firebase Studio supports creating web-based games and apps through natural language prompting, and integrates with Firebase's backend services including Realtime Database, Authentication, Cloud Functions, and Hosting. A notable Google Codelab walks users through 'vibe-coding' a kids game using Gemini and p
